text:
The fun and games of theParis Olympicshave come to an end andTeam GBhave won their final medals after a thrilling fortnight of action in theFrench capital.

Starting the day, Tigist Assefa, the women’s marathon record holder, looked to win gold forEthiopiabut was beaten to the title by theNetherlands’ Sifan Hassan – the 2023Londonmarathon winner – after a pulsating finish.

In the velodrome, Emma Finucane’s extraordinary Olympic debut continued with bronze in the women’s sprint, while Team GB team-mate Jack Carlin crashed out in a dramatic men’s keirin final.

British duo Kerenza Bryson and Kate French finished outside of the medal spots in the women’s modern pentathlon, with Blanka Guzi storming to victory at the Chateau de Versailles.

However, three years on from winning silver in Tokyo, Emily Campbell was back on the podium in the women’s +81kg weightlifting, which took Team GB’s overall medal total to 65 this summer.

All that remains is this evening’s closing ceremony, which kicks off at 8pm and represents a chance for Paris to pull down the curtain on a frenetic and action-packed two weeks across the Channel.

What an extraordinary Games it has been…

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

Paris 2024, it’s been a blast.

A big thank you from all the Metro team to everyone who has joined us these wild, unpredictable and at times golden last few weeks.

And remember, we’ve got the Paralympics just around the corner…

For now though, goodbye!

The ceremony isn’t quite over. Several of France’s stars who will be at the Paralympics in a couple of weeks’ time are handed a French flag.

And now there’s a rendition of Frank Sinatra’s ‘My Way’

And with that the Paris 2024 Olympics are officially over.

The flame is extinguished by athletes representing the five continents, Leon Marchand and Thomas Bach.

Bach is emotional as he declares the end. It’s the IOC president’s last Olympic games.

We cut live to a beach in LA where none other than the Red Hot Chili Peppers perform ‘Can’t Stop’ before Billie Eilish takes centre stage.

And now the main event. No not Daft Punk sadly, but it is the one and only Snoop Dog and Dr Dre. In some respects Snoop has been the standout star of these Olympics.

As expected, super spy Ethan Hunt – who poses as very popular actor Tom Cruise – ziplines into the stadium and claims the Olympic flag from Simone Biles.

He then rides off on a motorcycle with the flag before we go into a pre-recorded segment where he flies to LA and tours the city, including placing the flag onto the Hollywood sign.

Mission…. possible?Tom Cruise makes a stunning entrance from the roof of the Stade de France! ð¥#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/rJyoTJRCeo

Now the handover ceremony. The Olympic flag is passed over from Paris to Los Angeles.

And now for a taste of what the Americans will offer in 2028. Is that a certain Tom Cruise I spot?

Tom Cruise is seen during the closing ceremony of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.pic.twitter.com/xLR9yb4SnP

Thomas Bach, president of the International Olympic Committee: ‘These Olympic Games could only inspire the world, because our French friends prepared the stage. And what a magnificent stage it was.

‘Millions of people celebrating the athletes in the streets of Paris and all across France. Millions of spectators in iconic venues creating an overwhelming atmosphere. More than half of the world’s population sharing this spectacular celebration of the unity of humankind in all our diversity.

‘The Olympic Games Paris 2024 were a celebration of the athletes and sport at its best. The first Olympic Games delivered fully under our Olympic Agenda reforms: younger, more urban, more inclusive, more sustainable. The first ever Olympic Games with full gender parity.

‘These were sensational Olympic Games from start to finish – or dare I say: SEINE-sational Olympic Games.

‘The Olympic Games Paris 2024 were Olympic Games of a new era.’

Tony Estanguet, president of the 2024 organising committee, speaking to the athletes, says: ‘We knew you would be brilliant but you have been magic.

‘We are so proud of you, bravo to all of you.

‘We are all in love with the Games, the 19 days have passed so quickly and these 19 days will always stay with us.

‘The same venues in two and a half weeks time, 4,500 athletes are ready. I invite you on 28th August to the best return match of your lives, be there at the Paralympics 2024.’

Music over and sadly no Daft Punk (yet).

There’s a montage of how the Olympic flame got to Paris before Leon Marchand arrives in the stadium with it after his long walk from cauldron.

And now the speeches begin…

The theatrics are over and done with and now it’s time for some lovely French music.

Popular band Phoenix are the main performers but there’s a bit of chaos as some athletes ended up on stage with them. They’ve cleared now and the show goes on.

On a personal note, is there any chance of a Daft Punk comeback tonight?

From the sublime to the somewhat ridiculous.

The tens of thousands of volunteers who make the games actually possible receive their thanks and a huge round of applause as they should.

Then the theatrics begin, with a visually dazzling depiction of an alien landing on a now empty Earth lightyears in the future. They are introduced to what the Olympics are and what they stand for.

I think that’s what’s going on. Not entirely sure but it looks really cool!

Any thoughts on who the masked golden figure might be? ð¤#bbcolympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/YLM1bL7an5

Alex Yee wasn’t the only flag bearer to be caught out by that loose bit of carpet…

After a nice bit of karaoke, including Queen’s ‘We Are the Champions’ (because of course), all the athletes have settled in the stadium.

Now the medals are being presented for the women’s marathon which took place on Sunday morning. It’s the first time in history the women’s race has taken place after the men’s and been given more prominence at the games.

Sifan Hassan of the Netherlands won gold in an Olympic record time.

A loose bit of carpet is causing havoc in the stadium.

Alex Yee is one of the athletes caught out.

Careful Alex, don’t trip! ðHe can swim, cycle and run to triathlon gold, but carrying the flag in the closing ceremony is a different kind of pressure for Alex Yee ð#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/TORJcMc2lc

The magnificent Olympic cauldron has been extinguished but the flame lives on in a tiny lantern, which has been carried off by France swimming superstar Leon Marchand

The thousands of athletes who took part in the games are now entering the stadium, lead by their flag bearers.

Team GB are represented by gold medalists Alex Yee and Bryony Page.

The curtain is about to fall on the 2024 Olympics, with the closing ceremony now underway.

Unlike the opening, it’s taking place at the Stade de France. And thankfully it’s not raining!

Team GB Flagbearers and medallists Alex Yee & Bryony Page on how they’ll unwind as the Olympics draw to an end â¬pic.twitter.com/Diws3s28Xd

Alex Yee and Bryony Page are looking forward to letting their hair down after this summer’s Games come to an end.

The Team GB duo have been selected as Britain’s flagbearersat this evening’s closing ceremony at the Stade de France.

Yee won gold in the men’s triathlon, while Page completed her set of Olympic medals with gold in the women’s gymnastics trampoline competition.

USathlete Shelby McEwen has defended his decision not to accept a shared gold medal at the Games, which cost him the chance to become anOlympicchampion.

McEwen, competing in the men’s high jump final, was tied in first place withNew Zealand’s Hamish Kerr after clearing the 2.36m jump.

When both failed to clear 2.38m, they had had a choice to make: share the gold medal between them or compete in a jump-off. The pair agreed on the latter.

It backfired on the American however, as he lost the jump-off and had to settle for the silver medal.

Read thefull story here.

Jordan Chiles’ sister, Jazmin, says theUSgymnast has been subjected to sickening racism onsocial mediaafter she was forced to hand back the bronze medal she won at theParisOlympics.

‘The racism that’s behind the comments and vulgar and vile comments that are being made toward my sister is what I’m speaking on,’ she wrote on Instagram.

‘I’m saying it’s alive and well.’

Read thefull story here.

The USA have topped the final medal table for the Games after a dramatic victory in the women’s basketball final against hosts France.

Chinasat in first place with a total of 40 golds heading into the contest, with the USA just behind in second on 39.

It’s the 19th time in history that USA have topped the Olympic medal table. The last time they failed to do was at the Beijing Games back in 2008.

Read thefull story here.

title: Olympics schedule and results: Athletics, cycling and basketball | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 16 with athletics, cycling, modern pentathlon and basketball all on the schedule.
